# Memory Cache Driver

The memory cache driver stores data in memory. All cached data is lost when the application restarts.

## When to Use
- Local development
- Caching data for the duration of a process
- Fastest possible cache (no disk or network I/O)

## Best For
- Temporary data
- Caching API responses, user sessions, or computed values

## Limitations
- Data is lost on restart
- Not shared between processes or servers
- No eviction policy (see [LRU Memory Cache Driver](./lru-memory) for that)

## Alternatives
- [Memory Extended Cache Driver](./memory-extended): Sliding expiration (TTL resets on access)
- [LRU Memory Cache Driver](./lru-memory): Evicts least recently used items when full

## Options
- `globalPrefix`: Prefix for all keys (string or function)
- `ttl`: Time to live in seconds (default: Infinity)

## Example Usage
```ts title="src/config/cache.ts"
import { env } from "@mongez/dotenv";
import { CacheConfigurations, MemoryCacheDriver } from "@warlock.js/core";

const cacheConfigurations: CacheConfigurations = {
  drivers: {
    memory: MemoryCacheDriver,
  },
  default: env("CACHE_DRIVER", "memory"),
  options: {
    memory: {
      globalPrefix: "dev-app",
      ttl: 60 * 60, // 1 hour
    },
  },
};

export default cacheConfigurations;
```

## Storing and Retrieving Data
```ts
import { cache } from "@warlock.js/core";

await cache.set("user:1", { name: "Alice" }, 3600);
const user = await cache.get("user:1");
```

## Troubleshooting
- **Data disappears after restart**: This is expected. Use [File](./file) or [Redis](./redis) drivers for persistence.
- **Cache not shared between processes**: Use [Redis](./redis) for distributed cache.

:::note
Please note that the Memory Cache Drier implements all methods in [Cache Driver Interface](./cache-driver-interface) so you can use it directly as a cache driver.
:::
